problem_summary: 2021 MCM Problem C data type. From noisy AGH sighting reports in Washington State with only 14 confirmed positives, predict spread, classify mistaken reports, prioritize reports, and define an update plus eradication rule, under extreme imbalance and missing population data.
models_used: Suitability index from log population and barriers
Gaussian KDE for population and discredibility
Frozen VGG-11 plus SVM image classifier
key_techniques: Bayesian split into abundance image discredibility
AGH biology encoded in CA rules
Transfer learning plus augmentation on 13 images
Positive and negative reports both assimilated
why_it_won: One coherent pipeline answered all four sub questions and turned missing population data into a feature by inferring it from false reports. The interpretable Bayes rule gives the prioritization score and a threshold knob for officials. The work is honest and rigorous with temporal splits AUROC under imbalance adversarial look-alike tests and KL sensitivity all tied to AGH biology and an actionable severity-ranked memo.
section_highlights: A one-page summary names every method and headline numbers near 90 percent accuracy and 94 percent AUROC with a framework flowchart up front. Each model has a why-we-chose-this subsection and the Bayes math is from first principles. Spread maps overlaid on population are cross-checked against real cities like Seattle and Spokane. There is an honest weaknesses section an eradication decision tree and a severity-ranked memo to the Department of Agriculture.
pitfalls_or_limitations: Population proxy near-circular and visually validated only
Image metrics rest on a tiny set
CA parameters hand-set and unvalidatable long term
Some sensitivity figures degenerated in OCR
Text analysis was a dead end
reusable_patterns: Reconstruct missing covariate from data density
Frozen CNN plus classifier for tiny imbalanced sets
Temporal splits plus adversarial look-alike test
CA with seasonal rules and rare dispersal
Perturbation sensitivity with KL and intervals
Decision artifact with ranked table and tiered memo
